auth.title: The Workspace
auth.intro: The editorial desk for authors and their permanent record. Sign in to continue.
auth.footer: Access is provided by the publisher.

shell.notFoundTitle: There is no page at this address
shell.notFoundBody: Nothing in the record answers to it — the address may be mistyped, or the link may predate the page. Nothing has been lost.

authors.title: The Workspace
authors.intro: Each author kept here has a permanent, versioned memory. Conversations discover ideas; what is worth keeping is preserved here — so every future draft sounds more like its author, never more like AI.
authors.emptyRoster: No authors yet. The roster begins with its first author — add one to establish their permanent memory.

study.emptyBooks: No books yet. Every book opened here begins under this author’s established memory.
study.assembledMemoryHint: the exact record future AI assistance will receive — active, finalized versions only

form.addIntro: This opens the author’s permanent record. Their four memory documents — Writing Philosophy, Author Bible, Voice Profile, and Editorial Decisions — are created with it, ready to be established.
form.slugPlaceholder: derived from the name if left blank
form.editIntro: The author’s identity, as the imprint keeps it. The record’s address remains <address>/{slug}</address> — it was set when the record was opened and does not change.

overview.title: The Manuscript
overview.emptyManuscript: The manuscript begins with its first chapter. Your <link>Master Outline</link> already holds the shape — open it beside you and add the first chapter here.
overview.partsEmpty: Parts are optional. A manuscript without them reads as one sequence of chapters; add a part when the book needs larger movements.

form.addIntro: A chapter is the unit of authorship: it carries its own purpose and its own history. The words come later, in the chapter itself — this opens its place in the manuscript.
form.editIntro: The chapter’s identity, as the manuscript keeps it. Its address remains <address>/{slug}</address>; its number is always computed from its place in the manuscript.
form.title: Title
form.coreQuestionHint: the single question this chapter exists to answer
form.outlinePlaceholder: the part of the Master Outline this chapter serves

writingRoom.noBrief: No brief yet — give the chapter a purpose from its record.
writingRoom.nowAtVersion: the chapter is now at Version {number}
writingRoom.draftOpenContinueWriting: A draft is open for this chapter — <link>continue writing</link>.
writingRoom.newNextIntro: Starting from the current active version — write it into the next version. It is saved as a draft until you make it active.
writingRoom.activationNote: Activating finalizes the text permanently; discarding removes the draft. Neither touches earlier versions. Nothing is saved until you save it.
writingRoom.audioDraftNote: Reads the draft as last saved.
writingRoom.conceptsNotEstablished: The Concept Dictionary has not been established.
writingRoom.chapterContextHint: the exact record future AI assistance would receive for this chapter — active, finalized versions only

readingCopy.title: Reading Copy
readingCopy.emptyManuscript: The manuscript begins with its first chapter.

progress.inParts: in {count, plural, one {# part} other {# parts}}
progress.draftsOpen: {count, plural, one {# draft} other {# drafts}} open
progress.openFindings: {count, plural, one {# open finding} other {# open findings}}

errors.coreQuestionRequired: The chapter's Core Question is required — the single question it exists to answer.
errors.slugUnusable: A usable slug could not be derived from the title.
errors.chapterSlugTaken: This manuscript already has a chapter at “{slug}” — retitle slightly.
errors.chapterCreateFailed: The chapter could not be created.
errors.chapterSaveFailed: The chapter could not be saved.
errors.partCreateFailed: The part could not be created.
errors.chapterMoveFailed: The chapter could not be moved.
errors.contentRequired: A version needs content before it can be saved.
errors.contentRequiredToActivate: A version needs content to be activated.
errors.draftAlreadyOpen: A draft is already open for this chapter; continue writing it instead.
errors.draftCreateFailed: The draft could not be created.
errors.draftSaveFailed: The draft could not be saved.
errors.activateFailed: The version could not be activated.
errors.discardFailed: The draft could not be discarded.
errors.manuscriptMigrationMissing: The database is missing the manuscript migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260708000000_manuscript_foundation.sql (docs/setup.md §2).

documentRoom.readingSuperseded: You are reading a superseded version.
documentRoom.draftOpenContinue: A draft is open for this document — <link>continue editing it</link>.
documentRoom.emptyDraftOpen: A draft is open but nothing is active yet — <link>continue editing the draft</link>.
documentRoom.newFirstIntro: This will become Version 1, saved as a draft until you make it active.
documentRoom.newNextIntro: Starting from the current active version — edit it into the next version. It is saved as a draft until you make it active.
documentRoom.draftPrivacy: A draft is private working space. Nothing reaches the permanent record — or any future AI context — until you make it active.
documentRoom.activationNote: Activating finalizes the text permanently; discarding removes the draft. Neither touches earlier versions.
documentRoom.sourceNotePlaceholder: e.g. distilled from a voice conversation, July 2026

writing_philosophy.label: Writing Philosophy
writing_philosophy.description: What this author believes about writing itself — the principles that govern every decision below.

author_bible.label: Author Bible
author_bible.description: The permanent record of who this author is: history, themes, convictions, and worldview.

voice_profile.label: Voice Profile
voice_profile.description: How this author sounds: rhythm, vocabulary, sentence habits, and tone.

editorial_decisions.label: Editorial Decisions
editorial_decisions.description: Choices the author has committed to, recorded once so they are never re-litigated.

errors.fullNameRequired: The author's full name is required.
errors.slugUnusable: A usable slug could not be derived; please provide one.
errors.slugTaken: The slug “{slug}” is already in use.
errors.authorCreateFailed: The author could not be created.
errors.recordSaveFailed: The record could not be saved.
errors.contentRequired: A version needs content before it can be saved.
errors.contentRequiredToActivate: A version needs content to be activated.
errors.draftAlreadyOpen: A draft is already open for this document; continue editing it instead.
errors.draftCreateFailed: The draft could not be created.
errors.draftSaveFailed: The draft could not be saved.
errors.activateFailed: The version could not be activated.
errors.discardFailed: The draft could not be discarded.
errors.workflowMigrationMissing: The database is missing the Phase B workflow functions — apply supabase/migrations/20260703000000_author_memory_workflow.sql (docs/setup.md §2).

account.title: Account
account.intro: The platform as it presents itself to you. Preferences here shape menus, labels, dates, and platform instructions — never the words of any manuscript.

language.label: Interface language
language.explanation: The language of the platform's own chrome — menus, labels, dates, and instructions. This is not the language of any manuscript: each book states its own manuscript language on its record, and editorial reviews follow the book, not this setting.
language.onlyEnglish: English is the platform's interface language today. Another language becomes available here only when its complete interface and editorial workflow can be trusted.

shared.workspaceTitle: The workspace hit an unexpected error
shared.workspaceBody: Nothing in the permanent record has been lost. Try again; if the error persists, the server logs carry the details{digest, select, none {} other { (digest {digest})}}.
shared.adminBody: Administration reads live platform data; a query may have failed. Try again; if it persists, the server logs carry the details{digest, select, none {} other { (digest {digest})}}.
